name: Wait-Free Eras
summary: Nikolaev & Ravindran (PPoPP 2020) extend Hazard Eras to fully wait-free progress for arbitrary wait-free data structures, guaranteeing bounded reclamation work per operation. First universal wait-free SMR; helping mechanism on era publication; near-EBR throughput with strict bounds.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Nikolaev & Ravindran's **Wait-Free Eras** (PPoPP 2020) extend Hazard Eras to fully wait-free progress for arbitrary wait-free data structures by guaranteeing bounded reclamation work per operation. The key contribution is the first *universal* wait-free SMR scheme — any wait-free data structure can be paired with WFE and remain wait-free including reclamation.

The mechanism uses a **helping mechanism on era publication**: when a thread publishes its reserved era, other threads cooperate to ensure the publication completes in bounded steps even if the publishing thread is preempted. Achieves near-EBR throughput with strict per-operation bounds — important for hard-real-time and adversarial-scheduling settings where EBR's unbounded retention is unacceptable.
